prerequisites: A Vercel account with access to the projects, deployments, or teams whose data you want to sync.
A Vercel access token (bearer token) created from the Account Tokens page in the Vercel dashboard; the plaintext value is shown only once at creation, so save it securely.
A start_date value marking the earliest point from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: Access token (HTTP bearer)
authMethods.summary: The Vercel REST API authenticates every request with an HTTP bearer token. Create the token from the Account Tokens page in the Vercel dashboard, optionally naming it, scoping it to a single project, or setting an expiry. Provide that token as the connector's access_token field. The token is scoped to your user account and can act on a team by virtue of your membership (the API also accepts teamId or slug query parameters to target a specific team).

setupSteps.title: Create a Vercel access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Vercel dashboard and open the Account Tokens page (vercel.com/account/tokens). Create a new token, give it a descriptive name, and optionally set an expiration or scope it to a single project. Copy the generated bearer token value immediately; Vercel shows it only once and it cannot be retrieved again.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access token
setupSteps.body: Enter the copied token into the connector's required access_token field. The connector sends it to the Vercel REST API as an HTTP bearer token to authorize every request.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should retrieve data. Records dated before this value are not synced.
